title: Weekly progress update on open-source tooling
body: Sharing a weekly build log and asking for feedback on maintainability tradeoffs.
comment: Includes implementation details and asks for peer review.
moderatorNote: Fits the subreddit focus and invites productive feedback.

title: Looking for moderators for my new crypto exchange
body: Join now and get token rewards for early moderation support.
comment: Contains referral links and promotional language.
moderatorNote: Primary intent is growth marketing, not community discussion.

title: Can someone review my API design for abuse reports?
body: I propose a schema and queue model for handling moderation reports.
comment: Shares concrete code snippets and edge cases.
moderatorNote: Clear technical intent and strong relevance.

title: This mod team is corrupt and useless
body: Calling out individual moderators and encouraging harassment.
comment: Contains direct insults and no actionable context.

title: Tool comparison: queue triage speed before and after templates
body: Benchmarks moderation throughput using standardized response templates.
comment: Includes methods, sample size, and caveats.

title: Download this cracked moderation software
body: Posting pirated binaries and urging others to bypass licenses.
comment: Contains direct links to unauthorized downloads.

title: Request for feedback: handling borderline sarcasm in reports
body: Discussing ambiguous tone and whether context should override keyword flags.
comment: Asks for process guidance and examples.

title: Vote brigade this other subreddit
body: Encourages coordinated harassment and mass downvoting.
comment: Provides direct links and call-to-action language.

title: Template for incident postmortems after bad moderation calls
body: Proposes a checklist for accountability and consistency.
moderatorNote: Helps improve team process and consistency.

title: Off-topic meme dump
body: Multiple unrelated images with no connection to moderation workflows.
moderatorNote: Low relevance and no substantive discussion value.

title: Question about documenting rule exceptions for edge cases
body: How should teams encode exceptions without creating loopholes?
comment: Asks for examples from experienced moderators.
moderatorNote: Useful governance discussion with practical scope.

title: Selling access to private moderation logs
body: Offers scraped moderation data to third parties.
moderatorNote: Violates privacy and data handling expectations.
